div.head: 52. Memorandum From the Military Assistant to the President’s Assistant for National Security Affairs (Haig) to the Deputy Director of the United States Information Agency (Loomis)Source: National Archives, RG 306, Director’s Subject Files, 1968–1972, Entry A1–42, Box 1, INF–12 Public Opinion and Attitudes. Confidential.
div.p: The President has expressed an interest in learning who actually conducts the public opinion surveys USIA sponsors periodically in foreign countries. Specifically, he would like to know:
—whether the organizations contracted are subsidiaries of U.S. groups?
—if it is feasible to use organizations like Gallup and Sidlinger for this sort of survey?
Would you please send us a memorandum for the President on the above subject.

signed.affiliation:
Brigadier General, U.S. Army
Military Assistant
